insight: Mexico's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup shows that the team tends to lose a bit of momentum after these breaks, with an average change of -0.050 minutes per match. This suggests that the breaks may be disrupting Mexico's rhythm, causing them to slightly decline in performance after the pause. Notably, when Mexico is not pressing before a hydration break, they tend to lose momentum at the same rate, indicating that the break itself may be the main factor in this decline rather than the team's playing style. Overall, these statistics imply that Mexico may need to work on regaining their momentum quickly after hydration breaks to maximize their chances of success in the tournament.
